title: lippia elliptica
intro: Lippia elliptica, also known as Mexican oregano or Mexican marjoram, is a shrub-like plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include its potential to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Lippia elliptica is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
